docs.title: go-yaml
docs.text:
• Discussion #253 — Discussing v4 API Changes Central design thread for the next major version (22+ comments). This is where v4's error types, plugin surface, and option shape are being argued out, so most of the other items in this section trace back here. (updated: 2026-04-23T22:06:04Z, appearance #1)

  • #342 BC break: yaml construct error on empty/comment-only stream Backwards-compat regression: parsing an empty or comment-only document now raises an error. Breaks every consumer that treated "no content" as a valid load. (updated: 2026-05-05T15:58:34Z, appearance #1)

  • #345 Stack overflow when custom UnmarshalYAML wraps unmarshal callback error Recursive crash class triggered by a common error-wrapping pattern in custom UnmarshalYAML implementations. Any downstream library doing fmt.Errorf("...: %w", unmarshal(...)) can blow the stack. (updated: 2026-05-08T14:54:24Z, appearance #1)

  • #337 Folded block scalars gain one newline per yaml.Node round-trip Data drift bug: tools that load and re-save YAML via the Node API see folded scalars grow by a newline each pass. Round-trip safety regression that bites editing tools. (updated: 2026-04-21T20:49:31Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #332 Fix: KnownFields not being respected inside custom UnmarshalYAML() Active fix making strict-mode KnownFields propagate through nested decoders. Last touched today; addresses issue #321 and matters for any project using strict decoding. (updated: 2026-05-12T12:33:53Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #313 Plugin system with Limit plugin API (merged) Lands a plugin architecture in the v4 line, opening parser/emitter extension points. First user is a Limit plugin for resource caps, paving the way for billion-laughs-style DoS protection as a swap-in. (updated: 2026-03-14T16:04:22Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #319 Add DumpError for all dump stack errors (merged) Unified error type for the dump stage, paired with the previously merged LoadError (#311). Public-API-shape change feeding into the v4 error overhaul. (updated: 2026-05-01T21:33:38Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #340 Forbid AI co-author attribution in commits (merged) Project policy banning AI co-author trailers in commits. Notable governance signal for downstream contributors and a stance other YAML-org repos may follow. (updated: 2026-05-02T06:26:59Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: pyyaml
docs.text:
• PR #916 Fix exponential expansion based DoS in merge key processing Security fix for a billion-laughs-style amplification via duplicate alias references in merge keys. Worth tracking through to a release. (updated: 2026-05-05T09:43:06Z, appearance #1)

  • #576 PyYAML 6.0 load() function requires Loader argument The long-running 6.0 breaking-change thread (15+ comments). Still pulling traffic from users hitting the safe-load migration; useful pulse on how painful the 6.0 cutover remains. (updated: 2026-04-24T08:07:43Z, appearance #1)

  • #516 PEP to add yaml to standard library? Community discussion (12+ comments) about proposing YAML inclusion in the Python stdlib. Recurring high-interest topic that intersects with PyYAML's roadmap and maintenance story. (updated: 2026-04-03T07:03:24Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #930 Fix yaml.emit for percent-escaped tag prefixes Spec-conformance fix in the emitter for tag prefix percent-encoding. Edge case but matters for tag-heavy schemas and YAML 1.2 conformance. (updated: 2026-04-27T02:54:26Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #918 Fix off-by-one excluding U+10FFFF from valid Unicode range in emitter Correctness fix expanding the emitter's valid Unicode code-point range to the full plane. Small in code, real in semantics. (updated: 2026-02-20T09:52:08Z, appearance #1)

  • #924 Add riscv64 (linux_riscv64) wheel to PyPI releases Platform-support request with two open PRs (#926, #909) attempting to address it. Tracks the broader push to ship riscv64 wheels across the Python ecosystem. (updated: 2026-03-20T04:16:49Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: libyaml
docs.text:
• Release 0.2.6-rc.1 First pre-release in nearly 6 years (since 0.2.5 in 2020). Signals movement toward a long-awaited 0.2.6 stable; downstream packagers will care. (updated: 2026-04-24T12:21:49Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #329 Enforce nesting limit in scanner (merged) Hardening fix adding a nesting-depth limit in the scanner to prevent stack exhaustion on deeply nested input. Closes a long-standing DoS class for the C library. (updated: 2026-05-05T20:34:14Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #327 Add libFuzzer harnesses for parser, loader, scanner and emitter APIs Proposes broad fuzzing coverage across the public APIs. If accepted, this is the kind of infrastructure that surfaces a steady stream of robustness bugs (see #324 below). (updated: 2026-03-24T06:39:02Z, appearance #1)

  • #324 libyaml_dumper_fuzzer: parser leak on emitter init failure Fuzzer-discovered memory leak when the emitter fails to initialize. Real resource-management bug rather than a fuzzer artifact. (updated: 2026-03-08T21:48:47Z, appearance #1)

  • #325 compile error C4703: potentially uninitialized local pointer variable 'input' used MSVC build error with companion fix in PR #326. Matters for Windows packagers and embedded users on the MSVC toolchain. (updated: 2026-03-23T22:41:37Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: yq
docs.text:
• #2677 << short merge tags are not !!merge modified AND are now quoted, breaking some parsers Directly attributes a regression to bumping go-yaml from v4-rc.3 to rc.4. Merge keys now emit as quoted "<<" without !!merge, breaking downstream YAML 1.1 consumers. Most important go-yaml-adjacent issue in yq right now. (updated: 2026-05-06T18:18:39Z, appearance #1)

  • Release v4.53.2 First yq release shipping go-yaml v4.0.0-rc.4. Reference point for the regression report in #2677. (updated: 2026-04-17T06:28:18Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #2579 Bump go.yaml.in/yaml/v4 from 4.0.0-rc.3 to 4.0.0-rc.4 (merged) The dependency bump implicated in #2677. Worth a glance for what behavior changes between rc.3 and rc.4 leaked downstream. (updated: 2026-04-12T04:26:22Z, appearance #1)

  • #2054 yq is confused by the indentation of a comment, considers it belonging to a wrong node Reporter pins this on go-yaml's foot-comment placement logic. Long-running comment-association bug; representative of the class of round-trip fidelity issues in go-yaml. (updated: 2026-05-11T01:03:58Z, appearance #1)

  • #2608 A single string scalar as output is not quoted if necessary (breaking roundtrip safety) Encoder fails to quote scalars whose bare form re-parses as a map/sequence. Concrete round-trip safety hole; PR #2656 works around it by re-parsing via yaml.v4. (updated: 2026-04-10T10:18:44Z, appearance #1)

  • #1836 yq strips document separator when adding a comment Multi-document --- separator handling around head comments. Lives in go-yaml's encoder/emitter behavior for document markers. (updated: 2026-05-11T01:03:08Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: yaml/yamlscript
docs.text:
• #267 macOS arm64: installer Makefile uses cp libys*.so* (Linux glob) - fails on macOS Installer uses a Linux-specific glob that doesn't match macOS dylib naming, breaking installs on Apple Silicon. Real shipping bug, not a usage question. (updated: 2026-05-07T16:54:08Z, appearance #1)

  • #266 Blog post "YAML best practices": whitespaces after : and before # are not rendered Doc rendering bug stripping significant whitespace in a published blog post. Easy to fix; matters because the post is teaching YAML conventions. (updated: 2026-04-17T12:11:46Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: exercism/yamlscript
docs.text:
• #53 Two-Fer exercise validation failing to run Open issue reporting that the Two-Fer exercise validation is not running successfully. Blocks learners on a foundational Exercism exercise. (updated: 2026-03-05T00:08:55Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #54 bin/verify-exercises-in-docker Open PR adding a script to verify exercises inside Docker, aimed at reproducible local/CI validation. Probably needed to land before #53 can be triaged cleanly. (updated: 2026-05-08T11:42:04Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: yamlstar
docs.text:
• #2 Failed to build yamlstar==0.1.3 Open build failure report for the 0.1.3 release with active discussion (4 comments). Indicates packaging or build reproducibility friction users are hitting. (updated: 2026-03-31T18:02:46Z, appearance #1)

  • #1 Adopt official yaml test suite Tracking issue to integrate the official YAML test suite into yamlstar's test infrastructure. Core conformance work for a young YAML implementation. (updated: 2026-03-03T18:08:46Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: yamlscript-test-runner
docs.text:
• PR #44 Pin the Docker image (merged) Pins the Docker base image for reproducible builds. Merged today as part of a burst of test-runner Docker cleanup. (updated: 2026-05-12T15:39:51Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #43 Fix run docker (merged) Fixes the Docker run configuration for the test runner. Also merged today; pair with #44 above. (updated: 2026-05-12T15:38:31Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: www.yaml.org
docs.text:
• PR #182 Add yamltidy to the list of linters (merged) Adds the yamltidy tool to the site's catalogue of YAML linters. Small but visible: the site's lint listing is one of the top discovery surfaces for YAML tooling. (updated: 2026-03-25T22:33:51Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #180 Add serde-saphyr library entry (merged) Adds the serde-saphyr Rust YAML library to the libraries listing on the site. Signals continued Rust momentum in the YAML implementation ecosystem. (updated: 2026-03-16T03:59:10Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: gloat
docs.text:
• Release v0.1.37 Latest stable in a burst of point releases (v0.1.33 through v0.1.37 over the past week). Looks like an automated release pipeline doing its job; flag if cadence slows. (updated: 2026-05-10T17:56:21Z, appearance #1)

  • #4 Allow interop via generated proxy Open feature request proposing generated-proxy-based interop. No comments yet but the only open issue on the repo. (updated: 2026-03-04T18:13:02Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: glojure
docs.text: • Release v0.6.5-rc25 Latest release candidate in the v0.6.5 series, which has been cutting roughly one RC per day this week. No issues or PRs in the window; this is auto-cut RC traffic, useful only as a liveness signal. (updated: 2026-05-10T17:46:29Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: go-yaml
docs.text:
• PR #348 Fix !!merge tag regression for yq #2677 Upstream fix for the round-trip regression introduced by commit cb0eff8 where the implicit !!merge tag was no longer being elided on encode, producing output like !!merge <<: *x instead of <<: *x. Splits binaryTag/mergeTag handling so the implicit merge tag is stripped while explicit TaggedStyle preservation is kept. Directly unblocks the yq regression in mikefarah/yq#2677. (updated: 2026-05-13T13:06:37Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #347 [v3 backport] Replace gopkg.in/check.v1 with the standard testing library Backport of PR #52 onto v3, swapping the gopkg.in/check.v1 dependency for stdlib testing (+669/-336 across 7 files, 4 comments of active discussion). Trims an external dep from the still-widely-used v3 line. (updated: 2026-05-13T13:54:46Z, appearance #1)

  • #344 v3: corrupted godoc after gofmt in parserc.go, yamlh.go New v3 regression: commit 5f3d8c3 ran gofmt and mangled godoc layout in twelve flagged ranges across parserc.go and yamlh.go. Pure formatting damage, but it shows up in pkg.go.dev for one of the most-imported YAML libraries on the ecosystem. (updated: 2026-05-07T10:38:11Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: yq
docs.text:
• PR #2705 Fix for #2677 Downstream side of the go-yaml !!merge regression: this PR depends on yaml/go-yaml#348 and pulls the fix through into yq. The pair of PRs is what closes out the rc.3 → rc.4 bump fallout reported last week. (updated: 2026-05-13T13:11:18Z, appearance #1)

  • #2677 << short merge tags are not !!merge modified AND are now quoted, breaking some parsers Re-surfaced with fresh comments tying the breakage back to a specific go-yaml commit (fbd745b) and tracking the in-flight fix. Most important go-yaml-adjacent issue in yq right now; remains open until #2705/#348 land. (updated: 2026-05-12T22:02:05Z, appearance #2)

  • PR #2699 Fix nested inline YAML merge explode Fixes nested inline << merge-key handling under --yaml-fix-merge-anchor-to-spec (closes #2679; related to #2386 / #2400). The flag exists specifically to align yq with the YAML-spec merge semantics that go-yaml exposes, so this traces back to go-yaml merge-anchor behavior. (updated: 2026-05-08T15:33:03Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: pyyaml
docs.text: • PR #920 Fix ValueError/IndexError crash in SafeLoader for malformed numeric scalars (#898) Proposed fix for crashes on 0x_, !!float +_, !!float 1::3 and similar inputs, where construct_yaml_int / construct_yaml_float strip underscores without handling the now-empty string. Closed unmerged this week — worth a glance to understand the maintainer's preferred direction, since the underlying SafeLoader crash is real. (updated: 2026-05-07T09:10:58Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: libyaml
docs.text: • PR #314 Fix cmake deprecation Long-open PR (from 2025-06) saw fresh activity this week, likely in response to last week's merge of #331 (which bumped cmake_minimum_required to 3.5). Build-system territory; may be partially obsoleted now and ready to either rebase or close. (updated: 2026-05-12T07:43:31Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: www.yaml.org
docs.text: • #183 "YAML Test Suite" is listed twice on the main page Closed this week: the yaml.org homepage's YAML had a duplicate key, so the Symfony YAML parser errored with Duplicate key 'YAML Test Suite' detected at line 32. The canonical YAML site briefly shipping invalid YAML is a slightly embarrassing thing to have on the record; now resolved. (updated: 2026-05-09T14:51:03Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: gloat
docs.text: • Release v0.1.40 (and 8 intervening point releases) Nine new releases since v0.1.37 last reported — the automated cadence continues, but there is real feature content accumulating worth a single grouped mention. v0.1.35 added syntax highlighting to the browser REPL plus a Glojure WASM REPL page; v0.1.36 added --repl=VALUE client mode, --nrepl=path for editor integration, plus REPL share button / control toolbar / docs; v0.1.38 added Go interop docs and web pages; v0.1.40 adds --glj to print the bundled glj path and migrates deps docs to colon form / go-yaml. v0.1.31–v0.1.34 and v0.1.39 are small build/platform fixes (Plan 9, nospinbitmutex gating, stdin -t go fix). (updated: 2026-05-13T13:05:21Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: yamlscript-test-runner
docs.text:
• PR #45 Upgrade Docker image from Ubuntu 24.04 to Ubuntu 26.04 Open PR (by IsaacG, CCs @ingydotnet) bumping the test runner's base image two LTSes forward. Base-image upgrades can shift YAMLScript runtime behavior in the Exercism test runner, so worth a careful review before merge. (updated: 2026-05-13T05:51:35Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #42 Reduce apt-get size with a purge, cleanup and drop recommends Closed PR trimming the Docker image footprint by purging caches and dropping --no-install-recommends packages. Pairs with the broader Docker cleanup arc that landed in #43 / #44 yesterday. (updated: 2026-05-12T05:46:11Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #40 Bump actions/checkout from 4.2.2 to 6.0.2 Dependabot bump that crosses two majors (v4 → v6) on actions/checkout. Worth a glance since major-version jumps on this action have historically changed default behavior around submodules and credentials. (updated: 2026-05-12T05:45:36Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: yq
docs.text:
• #2677 << short merge tags are not !!merge modified AND are now quoted, breaking some parsers Still active: fresh comments on 2026-05-13 continuing to trace the breakage to a specific go-yaml commit (fbd745b), keeping pressure on the paired fix PRs #2705 and go-yaml#348. (updated: 2026-05-13T15:05:23Z, appearance #3)

  • PR #2705 Fix for #2677 The downstream yq-side fix for the !!merge regression; depends on go-yaml#348 and received further review activity today, moving closer to merge. (updated: 2026-05-14T09:20:04Z, appearance #2)

  • PR #2699 Fix nested inline YAML merge explode Fixes explode(.) behavior when merge keys are deeply nested ({<<: {<<: {a: 42}}}); received fresh review today, extending the YAML merge-key work that traces back into the go-yaml layer. (updated: 2026-05-14T09:33:51Z, appearance #2)

  • PR #2701 fix(json): preserve floats with trailing zero when encoding YAML to JSON Fixes loss of fractional part on !!float scalars like 50.0 when encoding to JSON; the bug traces to how go-yaml exposes float tags before Go's JSON encoder strips the .0. (updated: 2026-05-14T10:00:35Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: go-yaml
docs.text: • PR #347 [v3 backport] Replace gopkg.in/check.v1 with the standard testing library Active discussion (5 comments as of today) on the v3 test framework modernization; removing the abandoned gopkg.in/check.v1 dependency cuts an external dep from the still-widely-used v3 line. (updated: 2026-05-13T14:15:12Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: libyaml
docs.text: • PR #314 Fix cmake deprecation (merged) Long-pending PR (originally opened June 2025) finally merged on 2026-05-13, resolving CMake deprecation warnings for builds on CMake < 3.10. This closes out a minor but persistent friction point for projects building libyaml from source. (updated: 2026-05-13T17:39:21Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: makes
docs.text: • Add bun.mk + let-go.mk; fix python.mk, glojure.mk, gnat.mk Five direct commits to main this week by ingydotnet: new make modules for the Bun JavaScript runtime (bun.mk) and let-go (let-go.mk), a process-hanging fix in python.mk, GLOJURE_DIR env var support in glojure.mk, and a bug fix in gnat.mk. (updated: 2026-05-11T15:50:50Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: gloat
docs.text: • Releases v0.1.31 through v0.1.39 Batch of new releases this week covering a major REPL push: browser REPL with syntax highlighting, nREPL/socket REPL for editor integration (--nrepl=path), --repl=VALUE client mode, share button and control toolbar, Go interop docs, Plan 9 support, and a --glj flag to print the bundled glj path. (updated: 2026-05-13T02:52:12Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: yamlscript-test-runner
docs.text: • PR #45 Upgrade Docker image from Ubuntu 24.04 to Ubuntu 26.04 (merged) Merged on 2026-05-13, bumping the test runner's base image two LTS releases forward. Base-image upgrades can shift YAMLScript runtime behavior in the Exercism runner, so worth a sanity check. (updated: 2026-05-13T18:47:26Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: go-yaml
docs.text: • PR #348 Fix !!merge tag regression for yq #2677 Still open and actively updated today (timestamp advanced from 2026-05-13T13:06:37Z to 2026-05-15T07:11:35Z), confirming work is ongoing. The PR corrects an over-correction in commit cb0eff8: the implicit !!merge tag was being preserved on encode rather than elided, producing !!merge <<: *x instead of the correct <<: *x. Landing this is the upstream prerequisite for closing the paired yq fix in mikefarah/yq#2705. (updated: 2026-05-15T07:11:35Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: yq
docs.text: • PR #2705 Fix for #2677 Received a further update today (timestamp advanced from 2026-05-14T09:20:04Z to 2026-05-15T07:12:04Z), suggesting active coordination with the upstream yaml/go-yaml#348 fix. This PR cannot merge until go-yaml#348 lands first; both are moving together. (updated: 2026-05-15T07:12:04Z, appearance #3)

docs.title: makes
docs.text: • Add ttyd.mk; update 16 tool versions Two commits landed on 2026-05-14: a new ttyd.mk module bringing web-based terminal support to the makes toolset, and a broad version-bump commit updating 16 managed tools simultaneously (notable jumps include clang 19→22, node 25→26, and pulumi 3.232→3.238). (updated: 2026-05-14T14:00:43Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: gloat
docs.text: • Release v0.1.41 Minor patch released 2026-05-14 with no release notes beyond a changelog link, continuing the near-daily release cadence. Confirms the project is still actively shipping on top of the REPL and Go interop work that landed in the v0.1.35–v0.1.40 series reported earlier this week. (updated: 2026-05-14T21:33:09Z, appearance #1)

docs.text: • PR #348 Fix !!merge tag regression for yq #2677 Still in active development — updated twice on 2026-05-15, with the timestamp advancing again to 19:32 UTC (from 07:11 UTC seen in yesterday's report). A competing PR (#349, by W-Floyd) was opened and closed without merging this week, confirming the community settled on this PR's approach. Landing #348 remains the upstream prerequisite for yq PR #2705 to close out the rc.3→rc.4 merge-key regression. (updated: 2026-05-15T19:32:54Z, appearance #3)

docs.title: go-yaml
docs.text:
• PR #348 Fix !!merge tag regression for yq #2677 Advanced again on 2026-05-16, confirming continued work on the maintainer's chosen fix for the merge-key quoting regression introduced between rc.3 and rc.4. A community-submitted competing fix (PR #349, see below) was opened and closed this week, narrowing the path to this PR alone. Landing #348 remains the upstream prerequisite for yq PR #2705. (updated: 2026-05-16T14:21:27Z, appearance #4)

  • PR #349 Fix !!merge tag regression for mikefarah/yq#2677 (closed) A community alternative fix for the same !!merge tag regression; closed on 2026-05-13 in favour of the maintainer's approach in PR #348. Useful signal: community engagement on this bug is high and the ecosystem has converged on a single solution path. (updated: 2026-05-13T20:33:15Z, appearance #1)

Extends the REPL and Go interop infrastructure that has been landing steadily across the v0.1.35–v0.1.42 series. (updated: 2026-05-16T18:16:42Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: go-yaml
docs.text:
• PR #348 Fix !!merge tag regression for yq #2677 Advanced again on 2026-05-17 (timestamp 2026-05-16T14:21:27Z → 2026-05-17T13:02:39Z), confirming continued active work on the maintainer's chosen fix for the merge-key quoting regression introduced between rc.3 and rc.4. Still the upstream prerequisite for yq PR #2705; the pair appear close to resolution. (updated: 2026-05-17T13:02:39Z, appearance #5)

  • Discussion #253 — Discussing v4 API Changes The v4 API design thread saw new activity on 2026-05-17, with the timestamp advancing from 2026-04-23T22:06:04Z after weeks of quiet. This is the central venue for shaping go-yaml v4's error types, plugin surface, and option shape. (updated: 2026-05-17T09:47:47Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: go-yaml
docs.text:
• #344 v3: corrupted godoc after gofmt in parserc.go, yamlh.go Revived today with two new companion PRs: a prior gofmt commit (5f3d8c3) had mangled godoc comment layout across parserc.go and yamlh.go in both the v3 and main branches, corrupting what pkg.go.dev shows for one of Go's most-imported YAML libraries. (updated: 2026-05-18T18:32:11Z, appearance #2)

  • PR #351 v3: Revert incorrect gofmt changes New open PR (updated today) reverting the gofmt-corrupted godoc in the v3 branch; directly addresses issue #344 by partially reverting 5f3d8c3 and stabilising the comment formatting. (updated: 2026-05-19T07:54:07Z, appearance #1)

  • PR #352 Revert incorrect gofmt changes Companion to #351 applying the same godoc revert to the main branch, so both active lines are fixed together. (updated: 2026-05-19T03:09:11Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: glojure
docs.text: • #1 feature: support local packages via gljdeps.edn New feature request proposing :local/root-style local dependency support in gljdeps.edn, mirroring Clojure's deps.edn and Go's replace directive — would enable local-package development workflows without publishing to a remote registry first. (updated: 2026-05-18T17:36:36Z, appearance #1)

docs.title: go-yaml
docs.text:
• #345 Stack overflow when custom UnmarshalYAML wraps unmarshal callback error A crash-level bug: custom UnmarshalYAML implementations that wrap and re-return the error from the unmarshal callback trigger infinite recursion and blow the stack. Affects any library doing fmt.Errorf("...: %w", unmarshal(...)). Still unresolved with no fix merged. (updated: 2026-05-08T14:54:24Z, appearance #2)

  • #342 BC break: yaml construct error on empty/comment-only stream Parsing empty or comment-only YAML streams now raises an error in v4, breaking every consumer that previously treated no-content as a valid (nil) load. Unresolved and open. (updated: 2026-05-05T15:58:34Z, appearance #2)

  • #337 Folded block scalars gain one newline per yaml.Node round-trip Each load-and-save cycle through yaml.Node appends an extra newline to folded block scalars, corrupting data over repeated passes. Disqualifies go-yaml from use in format-preserving editing tools. Still open. (updated: 2026-04-21T20:49:31Z, appearance #2)

  • PR #332 Fix: KnownFields not being respected inside custom UnmarshalYAML() Open fix for a correctness bug where the KnownFields strict-mode option is silently ignored inside custom UnmarshalYAML implementations, potentially masking unknown-field errors in deeply nested decoding paths. Has been open and unmerged for several weeks. (updated: 2026-05-12T12:33:53Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: libyaml
docs.text:
• #325 compile error C4703: potentially uninitialized local pointer variable 'input' used MSVC compile error with a companion fix in PR #326 that also remains unmerged, blocking clean Windows builds. Especially relevant now that 0.2.6-rc.1 is out — packagers on the MSVC toolchain need this resolved before stable. (updated: 2026-03-23T22:41:37Z, appearance #2)

  • PR #327 Add libFuzzer harnesses for parser, loader, scanner and emitter APIs Still-open PR adding broad fuzzing coverage across the public API surface, relevant in the run-up to the 0.2.6 release. Fuzzer harnesses are the kind of infrastructure that surfaces safety issues before a stable cut. Awaiting maintainer review. (updated: 2026-03-24T06:39:02Z, appearance #2)

  • #324 libyaml_dumper_fuzzer: parser leak on emitter init failure Fuzzer-discovered memory leak: a parser resource is not freed when emitter initialization fails. Still unresolved and stale since March. (updated: 2026-03-08T21:48:47Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: yamlscript
docs.text:
• #267 macOS arm64: installer Makefile uses cp libys.so (Linux glob) — fails on macOS The installer's Makefile hardcodes a Linux-specific glob (libys*.so*) that fails on macOS where libraries use .dylib extensions, silently breaking installs on Apple Silicon. A one-line fix is proposed in the issue but remains unmerged. (updated: 2026-05-07T16:54:08Z, appearance #2)

  • #266 Blog post "YAML best practices": whitespaces after : and before # are not rendered The yamlscript.org blog post on YAML best practices drops significant whitespace around : and # in rendered code examples, making it look like those characters are disallowed — potentially misleading to new users learning YAML conventions. Still open. (updated: 2026-04-17T12:11:46Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: exercism/yamlscript
docs.text:
• #53 Two-Fer exercise validation failing to run A compile error in the two-fer exercise test file causes validation to fail with no subtests run, blocking learners on a foundational Exercism exercise. Open since March with no fix merged. (updated: 2026-03-05T00:08:55Z, appearance #2)

  • PR #54 bin/verify-exercises-in-docker Open PR adding Docker-based exercise verification that also tidies the two-fer test file — the direct fix path for issue #53. Still awaiting review. (updated: 2026-05-08T11:42:04Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: yamlstar
docs.text:
• #2 Failed to build yamlstar==0.1.3 Windows users report a build failure installing yamlstar 0.1.3 via uv pip install, traced to a missing Meta file in the sdist. Open with no fix or workaround merged. (updated: 2026-03-31T18:02:46Z, appearance #2)

  • #1 Adopt official yaml test suite Request to run the full official yaml-test-suite to improve YAML 1.2 spec compliance confidence. Still open with no PR linked. (updated: 2026-03-03T18:08:46Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: gloat
docs.text: • #4 Allow interop via generated proxy Open feature request for a code-generation tool that produces Clojure proxy wrappers for arbitrary Go libraries, enabling idiomatic interop without manual shims. Increasingly relevant given the Go interop documentation work that landed in v0.1.38 and v0.1.42 — but no traction on this issue yet. (updated: 2026-03-04T18:13:02Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: pyyaml
docs.text:
• PR #916 Fix exponential expansion based DoS in merge key processing Security PR: crafted YAML with duplicate alias references in merge keys can trigger exponential memory and CPU expansion (billion-laughs style). Open and unmerged after two weeks — any project parsing untrusted input with PyYAML remains exposed until this lands. (updated: 2026-05-05T09:43:06Z, appearance #2)

  • PR #930 Fix yaml.emit for percent-escaped tag prefixes Spec-conformance fix: the emitter incorrectly handles percent-encoded characters in tag prefixes, producing malformed output in round-trip scenarios involving custom tags. Open and awaiting review since late April. (updated: 2026-04-27T02:54:26Z, appearance #2)

  • #576 PyYAML 6.0 load() function requires Loader argument Long-running high-traffic issue documenting the 6.0 breaking change that made Loader mandatory in yaml.load(). Still attracting new comments from users upgrading from 5.x; a perennial reference point for the project's migration story. (updated: 2026-04-24T08:07:43Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: libyaml
docs.text: • Release 0.2.6-rc.1 The 0.2.6-rc.1 pre-release (published 2026-04-24) is still the latest libyaml release — no stable 0.2.6 has appeared in the four weeks since. Key change is a new 1000-level nesting limit (a potential breaking change for deeply nested documents) plus two parser bug fixes. Downstream packagers should keep watching for the stable cut. (updated: 2026-04-24T12:23:03Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: yq
docs.text:
• #2054 yq is confused by the indentation of a comment, considers it belonging to a wrong node Long-standing bug where go-yaml's comment-association logic misattributes comments to the wrong YAML node based on indentation, causing round-trip fidelity failures in tools that read and rewrite YAML. No upstream fix landed; still open. (updated: 2026-05-11T01:03:58Z, appearance #2)

  • #1836 yq strips document separator when adding a comment When yq adds a head comment, the --- document separator is silently dropped — a round-trip regression rooted in how go-yaml handles the document start marker alongside head comments. Still open with no resolution. (updated: 2026-05-11T01:03:08Z, appearance #2)

  • #2608 A single string scalar as output is not quoted if necessary (breaking roundtrip safety) Scalar quoting hole: yq emits bare strings that re-parse as different YAML types (maps, sequences, booleans), tracing back to go-yaml's scalar style decisions. PR #2656 worked around it but the underlying issue remains open. (updated: 2026-04-10T10:18:44Z, appearance #2)

docs.title: makes
docs.text: • Shell hardening: always use bash with pipefail enabled Two commits landed on 2026-05-20: .SHELLFLAGS is now set to -e -o pipefail -c so any non-zero exit in a pipeline fails the recipe immediately, and the conditional guard that could let non-bash shells bypass both SHELL and .SHELLFLAGS has been removed. All recipes now run unconditionally under bash with strict error handling. (updated: 2026-05-20T13:31:25Z, appearance #3)

docs.title: go-yaml
docs.text: • PR #340 Forbid AI co-author attribution in commits Still open after three weeks: a project policy PR to ban AI co-author trailers (e.g. Co-Authored-By: Claude) in commits to the go-yaml repository. Notable governance stance — any AI-assisted contributor workflow touching go-yaml should be aware of this policy before landing. (updated: 2026-05-02T06:26:59Z, appearance #2)
